bird.name: Common nighthawk

relations.subject: Common nighthawk
relations.evidence: |last2=Wilson |first2=Scott |last3=Mitchell |first3=Greg |date=2017 |title=Assessing the relative use of clearcuts, burned stands, and wetlands as breeding habitat for two declining aerial insectivores in the boreal forest |journal=Forest Ecology and Management |volume=386 |pages=62–70 |doi=10.1016/j.foreco.2016.11.026|bibcode=2017ForEM.386...62F }} The common nighthawk is drawn into urban built-up areas by insects. The common nighthawk is the only nighthawk occurring over the majority of northern North America
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: Common nighthawk
relations.evidence: Lack of flat roofs, pesticides, increased predation and loss of habitat are noted factors of their decline
relations.object_summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

relations.subject: Common nighthawk
relations.evidence: Further unstudied potential causes of decline include climate change, disease, road kills, man-made towers (posing aerial hazards), and parasites. The absence of flat roofs (made with gravel) in urban settings is an important cause of decline
relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。
